message:
fix(orchestration): skip missing worktrees and add velocity division guard

Two improvements:

1. start_next_queued_session: Instead of returning error when worktree
   is not found, mark the repo as error and continue to the next pending
   repo. This prevents the queue from stalling on a single missing
   worktree. (Fixes bd-f2gf)

2. calculate_output_velocity: Add guard against division by zero when
   window parameter is 0 or negative.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-qc9f","title":"Fix out-var assignments shadowed by locals","description":"After removing Bash 4.3+ namerefs, out-var helper _set_out_var uses printf -v. Several functions declared locals named like host/owner/repo/url/branch/event_type/path/repo_id, which shadow common caller variable names and prevent out-vars from being set (breaking parsing and stream-json event parsing).\n\nFix by renaming internal locals to uncommon __ru_* names and ensuring tests extract the needed helper functions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T09:17:44.765284158-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T09:18:09.952867969-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T09:18:09.952867969-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed: renamed internal locals to avoid out-var shadowing; updated parsing tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qc9f","depends_on_id":"bd-szcn","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-05T09:17:44.779986767-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qhw3","title":"Implement real-time progress display for agent-sweep","description":"Implements progress display system for agent-sweep operations with multiple repos.

## Background

When running agent-sweep across multiple repositories, users need clear feedback about:
- Which repo is currently being processed
- What phase the agent is in
- Overall progress (X of Y repos complete)
- Estimated time remaining (optional)
- Any warnings or issues encountered

## Implementation

### Progress Display Modes

1. **Interactive Mode** (TTY detected)
   - Use gum spinner for active operations
   - Show progress bar with repo count
   - Live update current phase/status
   - Color-coded status indicators

2. **Non-Interactive Mode** (--non-interactive or no TTY)
   - Print phase transitions as they happen
   - One line per significant event
   - Machine-parseable timestamps

3. **Verbose Mode** (--verbose)
   - Show agent output in real-time (streaming)
   - Print all ntm interactions
   - Detailed timing information

### Progress Components

```bash
# Example interactive display
[2/5] owner/repo-name
  └─ Phase 2: Generating commit plan... ⏳
  └─ Phase 1: Understanding ✓ (45s)
  └─ Preflight checks ✓

Progress: ████████░░░░░░░░ 40% (2/5 repos)
```

### Implementation Details

1. Use `gum spin` for spinners when available
2. Fall back to simple text updates without gum
3. Track timing per phase for summary
4. Buffer output to prevent interleaving with agent output
5. Support ANSI escape sequences for terminal control

## Stream Separation

Per AGENTS.md rules:
- All progress output goes to STDERR
- STDOUT reserved for JSON output in --json mode

## Related Beads

- Part of: bd-rhea (print_agent_sweep_summary)
- Parent epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Interactive mode shows spinner and progress bar
- [ ] Non-interactive mode prints clean status lines
- [ ] Verbose mode streams agent output
- [ ] Progress survives agent timeouts/errors gracefully
- [ ] Works with and without gum installed
- [ ] All output to stderr, stdout clean for --json","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:22:22.58904565-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T20:48:24.041355644-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T20:48:24.041355644-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented progress display with 10 functions for interactive/non-interactive modes, progress bar, ETA, and phase tracking. Integrated into run_sequential_agent_sweep().","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qhw3","depends_on_id":"bd-kczb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:36.128989169-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -8766,7 +8766,11 @@ calculate_output_velocity() {
         chars_added=0
     fi
 
-    # Velocity = chars / window
+    # Velocity = chars / window (guard against division by zero)
+    if [[ $window -le 0 ]]; then
+        echo "0"
+        return 0
+    fi
     local velocity=$((chars_added / window))
     echo "$velocity"
 }

+        # Load and start driver

+            REVIEW_DRIVER_LOADED=1

-    # Load and start driver
-    if [[ -z "${REVIEW_DRIVER_LOADED:-}" ]]; then

+        if ! driver_start_session "$wt_path" "$session_id" "$prompt"; then
+            log_error "Failed to start session for $repo_id"
+            update_review_state ".repos[\"$repo_id\"].status = \"error\""
+            continue

-    # Start session via driver

-        log_error "Failed to start session for $repo_id"

-        return 1

+        update_review_state ".repos[\"$repo_id\"].status = \"in_progress\" | .repos[\"$repo_id\"].session_id = \"$session_id\""

@@ -9889,6 +9898,16 @@ validate_agent_command() {
     cmd="${cmd#"${cmd%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
     cmd="${cmd%"${cmd##*[![:space:]]}"}"
 
+    # Reject newline-separated commands to prevent chaining via line breaks.
+    if [[ "$cmd" == *$'\n'* || "$cmd" == *$'\r'* ]]; then
+        jq -n \
+            --arg cmd "$cmd" \
+            --arg status "needs_approval" \
+            --arg reason "Command contains newline separators" \
+            '{command: $cmd, status: $status, reason: $reason}'
+        return 2
+    fi
+
     # Extract the base command (first token)
     local base_cmd="${cmd%%[[:space:]]*}"
     if [[ -z "$base_cmd" ]]; then
@@ -9902,7 +9921,7 @@ validate_agent_command() {
 
     # Security: Check for shell metacharacters that enable command chaining/injection
     # These could bypass base_cmd checks by executing additional commands
-    # Characters: ; | & ` $( ) && || (newlines handled by caller typically)
+    # Characters: ; | & ` $( ) && || (newlines handled above)
     if [[ "$cmd" =~ [\;\|\&\`] ]] || [[ "$cmd" =~ \$\( ]] || [[ "$cmd" =~ \&\& ]] || [[ "$cmd" =~ \|\| ]]; then
         jq -n \
             --arg cmd "$cmd" \
